one_liner: AI infrastructure spending is accelerating massively while power grid constraints loom, stocks sold off on inflation fears, and Anthropic's new flagship model launched at double the price of its predecessor.

top_themes.headline: AI data center building boom hits power grid limits

points.text: Broadcom, Apollo, and Blackstone are launching a $35 billion platform to build AI computing infrastructure, targeting over 20 gigawatts of capacity by 2028.

jargon.definition: a measure of electrical power; one gigawatt can power roughly 750,000 homes

points.text: Anthropic has an internal roadmap targeting 80 gigawatts of computing power, and total data center leasing this year could reach 30 gigawatts globally.
The US electricity grid is forecast to run out of spare capacity by 2027, meaning it cannot meet the power needs of new data centers being built.
By 2028, over half of new data centers may need to generate their own power on-site using gas, bypassing the public grid entirely.

jargon.definition: power generated on-site by the building itself, not drawn from the public electricity grid

points.text: Microsoft is close to leasing 1 gigawatt of data center space in the US in the current quarter alone, and is building a 2 gigawatt region in Europe driven by demand from OpenAI and Anthropic.

top_themes.headline: Anthropic releases powerful new AI model at higher price

points.text: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5, a public version of its most capable model, priced at double the cost of its previous top model.
The model performs significantly better on complex, long-running tasks — one example was Stripe using it to rewrite a 50-million-line codebase in a single day.
Fable 5 has strict content filters that block entire categories of questions, including cybersecurity topics, routing those queries to a weaker model instead.
After 14 days, subscribers must pay extra usage credits on top of their existing subscription to continue accessing the model.

jargon.definition: prepaid tokens or units you buy to pay for how much you use an AI model, on top of any subscription fee

points.text: OpenAI is reportedly preparing a similarly capable model, so it is too early to say Anthropic has a lasting performance advantage.

top_themes.headline: Stocks sold off as inflation fears and Iran war weigh

points.text: The S&P 500 fell 1.6% on Wednesday, with chip stocks hit hardest and oil prices rising on renewed US-Iran tensions.
The market is now very sensitive to rising government bond yields — even a small rise in yields has been causing large stock market drops in recent months.

jargon.definition: the interest rate the US government pays on its debt; when yields rise, borrowing costs across the whole economy tend to go up

points.text: A surprisingly strong jobs report last Friday pushed yields sharply higher, causing the Nasdaq 100 to fall 4.8% — its worst day since the April 2025 tariff panic.
Headline inflation was expected to come in at 4.2% year-on-year for May; a number above that level risks triggering another sell-off.
Oracle shares fell 6% after hours after it reported higher-than-expected spending on data centers, which weighed on its profits.

chart.title: Recent index declines from June 2 record highs

top_themes.headline: SpaceX IPO massively oversubscribed, set to price today

points.text: Demand for SpaceX shares is more than four times the number available, making it the most anticipated stock market listing in recent memory.

jargon.definition: when investors want to buy more shares in a new listing than are actually being sold

points.text: SpaceX is offering 555.6 million shares at $135 each, which would make this the largest IPO ever.

jargon.definition: initial public offering — the first time a company sells its shares to the general public on a stock exchange

points.text: The sheer size of the deal could stress behind-the-scenes market plumbing because index funds may need to buy roughly 30% of the shares available.

jargon.definition: funds that automatically buy shares in every company that belongs to a market index like the S&P 500; they must buy SpaceX shares if it qualifies for inclusion

points.text: SpaceX's Starlink internet service faces a regulatory delay in India due to national security concerns related to the Iran war.

top_themes.headline: AI model price war brewing between OpenAI and Anthropic

points.text: OpenAI is considering steep price cuts to its AI services, anticipating that Anthropic will do the same to win business customers.
Both companies are already losing billions of dollars because computing costs are so high, so a price war would put further pressure on their finances.
The tension is heightened because both companies are preparing to go public and need to demonstrate strong revenue growth to attract investors.
Anthropic's Fable 5 includes a restriction that prevents other AI developers from using it to help build competing AI models — a move that could limit rivals.

positions.name: Microsoft
positions.notes: SemiAnalysis reports Microsoft has leased nearly 1 gigawatt of data center space in the US so far in the current quarter, matching its first-quarter pace. It is also building a 2 gigawatt data center region in Europe to support demand from OpenAI and Anthropic, with roughly 1 gigawatt already signed with leading operators in Q2.

positions.name: Meta Platforms
positions.notes: SemiAnalysis believes Meta is actively leasing gigawatts of data center capacity. It is suspected to be the customer behind 810 megawatts of deals signed with Applied Digital in Q2, and has likely leased a 900 megawatt behind-the-meter site near Columbus, Ohio, which would expand its AI computing cluster to 3 gigawatts total.

positions.name: Amazon
positions.notes: Amazon has strengthened its advertising business by combining its shopping data with premium streaming TV inventory. Advertisers can now use Amazon purchase signals to target Netflix viewers, and Amazon has integrated LinkedIn's member data into its advertising platform, giving business-to-business marketers detailed audience targeting on streaming TV.
